violations.claim: REACHABLE: containsReturn traverses into nested function scopes
violations.smt2:
; REACHABLE: containsReturn traverses into nested function scopes
; A return inside an arrow function inside the guard does not early-return the outer fn
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 269
(declare-const guard_condition_true Int)       ; 1 = guard evaluated true
(declare-const nested_fn_has_return Int)       ; 1 = guard body contains fn()=>{ return; }
(declare-const containsReturn_result Int)      ; 1 = containsReturn says true
(declare-const outer_fn_did_return Int)        ; 1 = outer function actually returned early
(declare-const log_executed Int)               ; 1 = log executes
(declare-const neg_guard_added Int)            ; 1 = !(guard) added to path conditions

; Code transition: containsReturn recurses through ALL namedChildren including
; arrow_function and function_expression nodes — it has no scope boundary check
(assert (= nested_fn_has_return 1))
(assert (=> (= nested_fn_has_return 1) (= containsReturn_result 1)))

; Semantic reality: a return inside a nested function does NOT escape the outer function
(assert (=> (= nested_fn_has_return 1) (= outer_fn_did_return 0)))

; Code transition: containsReturn=true => !(guard) pushed to conditions
(assert (=> (= containsReturn_result 1) (= neg_guard_added 1)))

; Execution: guard=true, outer fn did NOT return => log executes
(assert (= guard_condition_true 1))
(assert (= outer_fn_did_return 0))
(assert (=> (= outer_fn_did_return 0) (= log_executed 1)))

; Violation: conditions claim !(guard) but log runs with guard=true
(assert (= guard_condition_true 1))
(assert (= log_executed 1))
(assert (= neg_guard_added 1))
(check-sat)

clause_history.clause:
; REACHABLE: containsReturn traverses into nested function scopes
; A return inside an arrow function inside the guard does not early-return the outer fn
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 269
(declare-const guard_condition_true Int)       ; 1 = guard evaluated true
(declare-const nested_fn_has_return Int)       ; 1 = guard body contains fn()=>{ return; }
(declare-const containsReturn_result Int)      ; 1 = containsReturn says true
(declare-const outer_fn_did_return Int)        ; 1 = outer function actually returned early
(declare-const log_executed Int)               ; 1 = log executes
(declare-const neg_guard_added Int)            ; 1 = !(guard) added to path conditions

; Code transition: containsReturn recurses through ALL namedChildren including
; arrow_function and function_expression nodes — it has no scope boundary check
(assert (= nested_fn_has_return 1))
(assert (=> (= nested_fn_has_return 1) (= containsReturn_result 1)))

; Semantic reality: a return inside a nested function does NOT escape the outer function
(assert (=> (= nested_fn_has_return 1) (= outer_fn_did_return 0)))

; Code transition: containsReturn=true => !(guard) pushed to conditions
(assert (=> (= containsReturn_result 1) (= neg_guard_added 1)))

; Execution: guard=true, outer fn did NOT return => log executes
(assert (= guard_condition_true 1))
(assert (= outer_fn_did_return 0))
(assert (=> (= outer_fn_did_return 0) (= log_executed 1)))

; Violation: conditions claim !(guard) but log runs with guard=true
(assert (= guard_condition_true 1))
(assert (= log_executed 1))
(assert (= neg_guard_added 1))
(check-sat)
